# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function fetchPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');

        if (filter_var($url, FILTER_VALIDATE_URL) === false) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 422);
        }

        $curl = curl_init();
        curl_setopt_array($curl, [
            CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
            CURLOPT_URL => $url,
            CURLOPT_USERAGENT => 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; YourAppName/1.0)',
            CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION => true,
            CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 10,
        ]);

        $response = curl_exec($curl);
        $err = curl_error($curl);
        curl_close($curl);

        if ($err) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch the URL'], 500);
        }

        $dom = new \DOMDocument();
        @$dom->loadHTML($response);

        $title = '';
        $metaTags = $dom->getElementsByTagName('meta');
        foreach ($metaTags as $tag) {
            if ($tag->getAttribute('property') === 'og:title') {
                $title = $tag->getAttribute('content');
                break;
            }
        }

        $bodyContent = $dom->saveHTML($dom->getElementsByTagName('body')->item(0));

        return response()->json([
            'title' => $title ?: $dom->getElementsByTagName('title')->item(0)->nodeValue,
            'content' => $bodyContent
        ]);
    }
}
